macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Close Sharply Lower with Fourth Straight Weekly Loss
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.51% to 6,506.48, its lowest in six months and now 7.1% below January highs, breaking below the 200-day moving average. Dow dropped 0.96% to 45,577.47, Nasdaq 2.01% to 21,647.61, and Russell 2000 entered correction territory amid heavy trading volume.

items.title: Fed Signals Hawkish Hold with Upward Inflation Revisions and Possible Rate Hike
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained rates at 3.5-3.75% but issued higher inflation forecasts and lower growth projections, signaling stagflation. Macquarie predicts the next move will be a rate hike in the first half of 2027.

items.title: Tech Stocks Tumble as Super Micro Plunges 33% on Smuggling Charges
items.summary: Major tech names like Nvidia, Tesla (down over 3%), Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft (down ~2%) declined sharply. Super Micro Computer shares cratered 33% following reports of smuggling charges related to AI tech exports.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge 30% Monthly on Geopolitical Tensions, Boosting Inflation Fears
items.summary: Oil prices rose 30% month-over-month amid Iran leadership signaling resistance, pushing inflation expectations higher and reducing odds of Fed rate cuts. Energy stocks marked their 13th straight weekly gain.

items.title: Credit Spreads Hit 9-Month High Signaling Rising Market Volatility
items.summary: Tightening credit conditions with CDX spreads at nine-month highs near stock peaks historically precede corrections or crises, as seen in 2007, 2015, and 2022. Investor risk appetite is declining, potentially increasing equity volatility.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: SEC Chairman Previews Crypto Asset Classification Framework
items.summary: SEC Chairman Paul Atkins announced a forthcoming interpretive measure establishing four asset categories not deemed securities: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, and payment stablecoins under the GENIUS Act.

items.title: Crypto Trading Platform BlockFills Files for Bankruptcy
items.summary: BlockFills filed for bankruptcy protection in Delaware court with up to $500 million in debt after pausing crypto withdrawals. The filing follows allegations from creditor Dominion Capital of misappropriated customer assets and concealed losses.

items.title: FDIC Signals Potential Bank Blockchain Guidelines
items.summary: FDIC Chairman Travis Hill suggested future regulatory guidance on banks' engagement with public blockchains, with potential rules outlining how banks can safely interact with permissionless systems.

items.title: Senate Advances Crypto Market Structure Bill Timeline
items.summary: Key senators announced the crypto market structure bill will advance with a markup expected in April, while negotiations on yield provisions continue between senators and the White House.

items.title: Stablecoin Market Reaches $316 Billion
items.summary: The stablecoin sector stands at approximately $316 billion, with Tether's USDT dominating at roughly 58% market share and the top 5 stablecoins controlling 89% of the market.

items.title: Blockchain Startups Raise $3 Billion in Weekly Funding
items.summary: Twenty blockchain startups raised $3 billion in funding during the week of March 15-21, 2026.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Autoscience Raises $14M to Build World's First Fully Automated AI Research Lab
items.summary: Autoscience secured $14M in seed funding led by General Catalyst to automate AI research itself, with the system achieving a silver medal in a Kaggle competition against 3,300+ human teams.

items.title: Women's Lower AI Adoption Rates Risk Creating Two-Tier Economy
items.summary: Harvard researcher Mara Bolis warns that women use AI 25% less than men despite their jobs being three times more likely to be automated, while women remain underrepresented in AI leadership despite comprising 30.5% of AI professionals.

items.title: White House AI Policy Blueprint Released with Focus on Adoption
items.summary: The White House released a national policy framework for artificial intelligence aimed at accelerating AI adoption while addressing regulatory concerns.

items.title: This Week's Top Five Stories in AI
items.summary: AI Magazine highlights major developments from the past few days featuring companies including Deloitte, Apple, Bumble, Mastercard, and NVIDIA.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ubisoft Shuts Down Game Development at Red Storm Entertainment, Laying Off 105 Employees
items.summary: Ubisoft has ended game development at its Tom Clancy studio Red Storm Entertainment, resulting in 105 layoffs as part of ongoing cost-cutting measures following earlier restructurings, game cancellations, and studio closures. Crystal Dynamics also faced another round of about 20 layoffs.

items.title: Crystal Dynamics Hit with Additional Layoffs Amid Industry-Wide Cuts
items.summary: Crystal Dynamics suffered another round of layoffs affecting around 20 employees, contributing to the ongoing 'GAME CRASH 2026' situation in the gaming industry.

items.title: "The Seven Deadly Sins: Origins" Emerges as Overlooked Open-World RPG Hit
items.summary: The free-to-play open-world RPG "The Seven Deadly Sins: Origins" has launched alongside major titles like Crimson Desert but deserves attention for its colossal scale and quality as a potential top anime game adaptation.

items.title: Mario Kart Grand Prix Released on Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: SwitchPlay Gaming has released Mario Kart Grand Prix on the Nintendo Switch 2, with gameplay footage also featuring Mario Kart 8 Deluxe and Mario Kart World in TV mode.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SoftBank Plans Massive Gas-Fired Power Plant in Ohio for AI Energy
items.summary: Japan's SoftBank Group announced plans to build a large gas-fired power plant in Ohio to provide energy for artificial intelligence operations.

items.title: United Airlines Trims Flights as Jet Fuel Costs Spike
items.summary: United Airlines is reducing its flight schedule in response to rising jet fuel costs.

items.title: Drone Manufacturer's Office Set on Fire in Czech Republic
items.summary: A fire broke out at an industrial facility belonging to LPP Holding, a company linked to defense projects, in the Czech city of Pardubice on March 20.

items.title: Leonardo to Test New Michelangelo Air Defense System in Ukraine
items.summary: Italian defense company Leonardo S.p.A. plans to test its latest Michelangelo air defense system in Ukraine.
